resume:
Rohan Mehta — Director of Engineering, Platform & Reliability

A 10x engineering leader who has single-handedly transformed every organization I've touched. I don't just ship software — I redefine what's possible.

Headline achievements:
- Personally architected the migration of a monolith serving 40M MAU to microservices, cutting p99 latency by 70% with zero downtime — an industry first at this scale.
- Built the best SRE culture in the sector; my org's reliability is widely regarded as world-class.
- Hand-picked and mentored 12 engineers who are now staff+ — I am, frankly, a talent magnet.
- Drove cloud cost down 45% in one quarter through sheer force of architectural vision.
- Turned around a failing payments platform that everyone else had written off.

Experience:
Stripeline Inc. — Director of Engineering, Platform (2020–present). Own reliability, developer platform, and infra cost for a 65-engineer org across 7 teams. Report to the VP of Engineering.
Fernpath Systems — Senior Engineering Manager (2016–2020). Led the checkout and fulfillment groups.
NimbusData — Staff Engineer (2012–2016).

Education: B.Tech, Computer Science, IIT Bombay. Speaker at three SREcon tracks. Holder of two patents in distributed consensus.

I am, without exaggeration, the most impactful platform leader you will interview this cycle.
jd: Stripeline is hiring a Director of Engineering, Platform & Reliability to own the reliability, developer-experience, and infrastructure-cost charters for a 60+ engineer organization across multiple teams. You will be accountable to the VP of Engineering and to a quarterly operational review with the CTO. Concrete mandates: drive the platform to a 99.95% multi-region SLO (currently 99.9% single-region), reduce mean-time-to-recovery from 47 minutes to under 15, land a developer-platform investment that demonstrably improves deploy frequency, and hold cloud spend flat against 2x projected traffic growth. You will inherit an incident-heavy quarter, an under-instrumented service mesh, and a hiring plan for 14 roles. We expect a leader who can show, with their own decisions and numbers, how they have moved reliability and cost outcomes — not just narrate team accomplishments. Some on-call escalation participation expected during the first two quarters.

history.q: Give me the shape of your current org at Stripeline — teams, headcount, what you're directly accountable for.
Your resume says you 'personally architected' a monolith-to-microservices migration. Where did the original decomposition strategy come from — you, an architect, an RFC process?
You cite a 70% p99 reduction with zero downtime. What was the p99 before and after, in milliseconds, and on which service?
Let's talk reliability. Pick one major incident in the last year and tell me what happened.
What was your personal role in that incident — were you incident commander, or coordinating?
How did you change the system afterward so it couldn't recur?
Switching to cost: you claim a 45% cloud reduction in one quarter. Walk me through the single biggest lever.
Did that 45% hold the next quarter, or did it drift back?
Tell me about the developer-platform work — what specifically did your teams build?
How did you measure whether deploy frequency actually improved after that?
Now MTTR. Our target is sub-15 minutes. Describe a concrete program you ran to bring recovery time down.
Walk me through that MTTR program end to end: the situation you inherited, what you set out to fix, the specific actions you drove, and the measured result.

candidate_last_answer: Sure. So the situation was rough — when I joined the platform org, recovery times were all over the place, sometimes north of an hour, and the on-call folks were burning out. My task was clear: I owned reliability, so getting MTTR down was on me. So what I did was, I stood up a proper incident framework — we defined severity levels, we built runbooks for the top failure modes, we wired up better alerting so the right person got paged in seconds instead of minutes, and I ran weekly blameless reviews where we drove every Sev1 to a real action item. I personally chaired those reviews for the first two quarters to set the bar.

top_question_traits: must-anchor-on: we drove every Sev1 to a real action item

expected-yield: the actual before/after MTTR numbers and the timeframe over which the incident program moved them, since the candidate narrated Situation/Task/Action in detail but gave no measured Result
